Bug / Task Description: The keyword (BM25) retriever behaves badly when a query contains a very common word — one that appears in most or all of the indexed documents. In that situation genuinely relevant documents are scored so low that they get pushed below irrelevant ones, or dropped from the results entirely: for example, searching for 'the quantum ...' can return nothing even though one document is clearly about quantum topics. The problem is in how the retriever weights each query term by how common it is across the corpus. Fix the keyword retriever so that a common query term can never drive a relevant document out of the ranking, while preserving sensible ranking for rare and normal terms. Add a hidden behavioural test under tests/unit/ that captures this.
Commit Subject: fix(retrievers): keep BM25 IDF non-negative so common terms can't evict relevant docs
